tasks.description: Upgrade @oobe-protocol-labs/synapse-sap-sdk from v0.4.1 to v0.6.0 and create sap-client.ts singleton for use in live routes

review.summary: Clean implementation of SAP SDK v0.6 upgrade with proper singleton pattern and graceful error handling. Code follows TypeScript best practices and matches task requirements exactly.

issues.description: Missing JSDoc on type alias SapClient - consider adding brief description
Could benefit from unit tests, though not explicitly required for this task

strengths: Exact surgical edit to package.json - only changed the required dependency version
Proper lazy singleton pattern with _initAttempted flag to prevent repeated failures
Graceful error handling - returns null instead of throwing, preventing server crashes
Clear JSDoc documentation with environment variable descriptions
Proper TypeScript typing with extracted SapClient type
Follows project logging conventions with [sap-client] prefix
File is 42 lines - well under the 55 line requirement
No code fences or invalid syntax
Handles both missing file and JSON parsing errors appropriately
Uses proper Solana keypair handling with Uint8Array conversion

tasks.description: Add escrow settle step to sap-execute.ts — fire client.escrow.settle() after capability delivery (was missing, escrow was verified but never claimed)

review.summary: File is truncated and incomplete. The code cuts off mid-line in the switch statement, making it syntactically invalid. This appears to be a generation error where the full file content was not provided.

issues.description: File is truncated at line 'error: { message: 'payment:invoice requires invoiceNumber and amount', code: 'INVALID_P' - this is syntactically invalid and would not compile
Missing the complete switch statement implementation, missing the settle call that was the core requirement of this task
Cannot verify if the required surgical edits were properly implemented due to truncation
Missing export statement and proper file closure

strengths: Correct imports were added as specified in SURGICAL EDIT 1
TypeScript typing appears correct in the visible portions
Error handling structure follows project patterns

tasks.description: Wire compliance:tax capability in sap-execute.ts to real AgentTax engine — replace stub with calculateAgentTax() call
Replace raw RPC getTokenAccountBalance with SDK-based escrow verification — fixes 402 rejection on all valid SAP escrows
tasks._integrityDetails: File backend/src/routes/sap-execute.ts shrank from 168 to 6 lines (4%). Possible destructive rewrite.

review.summary: Critical failure: Destructive rewrite detected. File shrank from 168 lines to 6 lines (4% retention), indicating complete loss of existing functionality.

File contains only an integrity check comment instead of actual TypeScript code - no implementation provided
Task requires surgical replacement of verifyEscrow() function only, but entire file was destroyed
No actual code changes made - task specification was completely ignored

strengths: Integrity check system correctly detected and prevented destructive rewrite
